user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed car and the rear bumper area with visible damage, meeting general object part and body panel requirements.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows severe denting and deformation in the claimed 'rear bumper area' and the trunk, which is consistent with the customer's description of a 'dent', even though the damage is much more extensive, including a missing bumper cover. The claim is supported because the core issue (dent/deformation) is present in the specified area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows significant damage to the front bumper of a car, meeting the visual evidence standard for the claimed part and damage type.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows damage on the front bumper, which includes scratches and dents, supporting the general claim of damage on this part, even though the damage severity is greater than 'scratch'. The presence of a wrong object (img_2) does not negate the clear evidence in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly displays extensive crack damage on the windshield, fulfilling the requirement for clear visibility of the claimed part and damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a severely cracked windshield, directly supporting the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the side mirror with extensive damage, meeting the requirements for inspecting cracks, breakage, and misalignment.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows the side mirror with extensive cracks on the mirror glass and the housing appears misaligned, supporting the claim of damage and that it is 'not sitting the way it should anymore'.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows damage on a part of the vehicle's rear, meeting the general requirement for visual evidence of claimed damage to the 'back' of the car, even if not precisely the 'bumper'.
claim_status_justification: While damage (dents) is clearly visible in img_1, it is on the rear fender/quarter panel, not the claimed 'rear_bumper'. Image img_2, which shows the rear bumper clearly, does not reveal any damage on the bumper itself. According to rule 7, the claim is contradicted if the claimed area shows NO damage at all, even if adjacent areas do.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claimed part (headlight) is not clearly visible in the image; it is obscured by intense glare from the sun and the angle of the photo makes it impossible to inspect for damage.
claim_status_justification: The image provided does not offer a clear view of the claimed headlight due to the angle and extreme glare, making it impossible to verify the presence or absence of a crack.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_2 clearly depicts a dent on the car door, which is the claimed object part and issue.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a significant dent on the door panel, aligning with the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows a car with its hood, fulfilling the general object and part visibility requirement.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a car with extensive, severe damage, including to the front end and opened hood, which is far beyond a simple 'scratch on the hood' as claimed. The claimed scratch is not discernible amidst the major damage visible. The car appears to be a wreck, contradicting the claim of a minor mark post-service.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image img_1 clearly displays the laptop and its screen with significant damage, fulfilling the requirements to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: The image provided clearly shows severe cracking and shattering across the entire laptop screen, directly supporting the customer's claim of a crack on the display.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed hinge area with visible damage, meeting the requirements for reviewing a laptop hinge claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a broken hinge area, matching the customer's description of a 'broken hinge' and the screen wobbling.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop keyboard with visible water, fulfilling the requirements for inspecting the claimed part and damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows water spilled across the laptop keyboard, directly supporting the claim of water damage to this area.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent on the laptop lid near the corner, which aligns with the customer's claim and provides sufficient visual evidence to evaluate the damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly depicts a visible dent on the laptop's corner, directly supporting the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed laptop screen with visible damage, meeting all relevant minimum image evidence requirements.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a severe crack on the laptop screen, which is the claimed object part, supporting the customer's claim of screen damage. Although the customer described it as 'shattered,' the visual evidence aligns with the 'crack' definition as the screen appears intact without loose pieces.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop's palm rest area, adjacent to the trackpad, where the damage is claimed, meeting REQ_LAPTOP_BODY_HINGE_PORT.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a visible scratch on the laptop's body/palm rest area, which aligns with the customer's claim of "physical damage around the trackpad area" due to hitting the "front area" on a desk edge.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the package exterior and the claimed crushed corner, allowing for inspection of the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a crushed corner on the package, consistent with the customer's claim of a crushed package corner.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: REQ_PACKAGE_EXTERIOR: Image img_1 clearly shows the package exterior and the claimed sealed side with visible tearing and damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of significant tearing and damage to the package seal, directly supporting the customer's claim of torn packaging, despite img_2 showing an undamaged package.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows water damage on the package surface, meeting [REQ_GENERAL_OBJECT_PART], [REQ_PACKAGE_EXTERIOR], [REQ_PACKAGE_LABEL_OR_STAIN], and [REQ_REVIEW_TRUST].
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows extensive water damage with visible stains and softened, warped cardboard on the package side and corner, directly supporting the customer's claim of a water-damaged package with a wet-looking stain.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the opened package and its contents area, allowing for assessment of missing items, as required by REQ_PACKAGE_CONTENTS.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1, which depicts the inside of the opened package, shows only packing materials and no product. This visually supports the claim that the contents (the item itself) are missing from the package.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a product (can) with damage, not the claimed shipping package (outside box).
claim_status_justification: The customer claimed a crushed shipping box, but the provided image shows a dented product (can) instead of a shipping package. The claimed object is not present in the visual evidence.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (package) and the claimed part (seal) with the described damage (torn).
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a torn seal on the package, directly supporting the customer's claim that the seal area looked torn. Although img_2 shows an intact package, rule 8 states that if at least one image clearly shows the claimed damage, the claim is supported.
